summary:
There is no credible evidence linking head positioning to alterations in anterior chamber depth in pseudoexfoliation syndrome. The sole reference to facedown positions pertains to a rare case of pseudophacodonesis from iris chafing, lacking any demonstrated impact on anterior chamber measurement or dimension. 

\boxed{\text{No}}

\boxed{\text{No}}. Answer choice: B
